steps.title: Find the annual growth factor
steps.content: A 2.4% increase each year means the new value is $100\% + 2.4\% = 102.4\%$ of the previous year's value. In decimal form, the growth factor is $$1 + 0.024 = 1.024.$$

steps.title: Build the exponential model
steps.content: With initial value $\$174{,}000$ and annual multiplier $1.024$, after $x$ years the value is $$f(x) = 174{,}000 \cdot (1.024)^x.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $f(x)=174{,}000(0.024)^{x}$ — uses the rate $0.024$ alone, which would shrink the value. Incorrect.
  • Choice B: $f(x)=174{,}000(0.24)^{x}$ — uses $0.24$ (also less than $1$, so a decrease), and misreads $2.4\%$ as $24\%$. Incorrect.
  • Choice C: $f(x)=174{,}000(1.024)^{x}$ — uses the correct growth factor $1 + 0.024 = 1.024$. Correct.
  • Choice D: $f(x)=174{,}000(1.24)^{x}$ — corresponds to a $24\%$ annual increase, not $2.4\%$. Incorrect.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
